question: Are the surrounding trees transformed into cherry trees covered in dense pink and white blossoms?
Does the distant view and background skyline show masses of soft cherry blossoms?
Are there gently drifting cherry blossom petals visible in the mid-ground and background?
Is the foreground picnic area (blanket, basket, food, bottles) free from obscuring drifting petals?
Is the fountain preserved in its exact original position, scale, and structure (including water spray)?
Are the background pedestrians and people in the mid-ground retained in the scene?
Do the background pedestrians exhibit a subtle bokeh blur, rendering them softer than the foreground?
Is there a gentle highlight bloom applied to the image?
Is a faint pink-tinted lens flare visible in the image?
Is the red and white checkered picnic blanket preserved unchanged in appearance?
Are the foreground items (wicker basket, glass bottles, food on plates) preserved without modification or distortion?
Are the foreground items maintained in sharp focus (not affected by the background bokeh)?
Is the transition between the sharp foreground and the softer, blossomed background natural and seamless?
Is the lighting on the new cherry blossom trees consistent with the overall scene lighting?
Does the image maintain overall optical clarity without appearing washed out or muddy despite the bloom/flare effects?
Are the edges of foreground objects clean and free from background color spill or artifacts?
Is the image free of unnatural artifacts (e.g., repeating petal patterns, distorted anatomy, smearing)?
